problem: There are 3 math teams in the area, with 5, 7, and 8 students respectively. Each team has two co-captains. If I randomly select a team, and then randomly select two members of that team to give a copy of $\emph{Introduction to Geometry}$, what is the probability that both of the people who receive books are co-captains?

classified_questions.question: What is the main objective of this problem?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many math teams are there in the area, and how many students are on each team?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many co-captains does each team have?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If you randomly select a team, what is the probability of selecting each team?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Once you have selected a team, how many different pairs of students can you choose to give books to?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Out of all the possible pairs, how many of them consist of the two co-captains?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Once you have selected a team, what is the probability of giving books to the co-captains?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we express the probability of selecting a team, selecting two co-captains, and then giving books to them?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the probability for each team and then combine them?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final probability that both people who receive books are co-captains?
classified_questions.label: b
